concepts.name: Dataset Compilation
concepts.description: Reframing data engineering as a compiler pipeline that transforms raw observations into optimized, training-ready tensors.

concepts.name: Data Gravity
concepts.description: The constraint and cost of moving massive datasets across networks, calculated as volume divided by bandwidth.

concepts.name: Information Entropy
concepts.description: The density of useful signal or predictive information contained per byte of a dataset.

concepts.name: Feeding Tax
concepts.description: The wall-clock time lost and GPU efficiency reduced due to I/O wait from slow storage pipelines.

concepts.name: Energy-Movement Invariant
concepts.description: The fundamental physical constraint that moving data across networks consumes significantly more energy than computation.

concepts.name: Data Cascades
concepts.description: A compounding failure pattern where poor upstream data quality silently amplifies and causes complex downstream model failures.

concepts.name: Schema Validation
concepts.description: The synchronous enforcement of expected data types and value ranges to reject malformed records entering the pipeline.

concepts.name: Population Stability Index
concepts.description: A statistical metric used to quantify how much a categorical or binned feature distribution has shifted over time.

concepts.name: KL Divergence
concepts.description: An information-theoretic measure of distance between continuous data distributions used to detect model data drift.

concepts.name: Covariate Shift
concepts.description: A distribution shift where input feature distributions change while the feature-to-label relationship remains constant.

concepts.name: Label Shift
concepts.description: A distribution shift where the output label frequency changes while the relationship between labels and features remains constant.

concepts.name: Concept Drift
concepts.description: A distribution shift where the underlying functional relationship between input features and target labels changes over time.

concepts.name: Label Quality Drift
concepts.description: The gradual degradation in the reliability and consistency of ground truth annotations, independent of underlying data distributions.

concepts.name: Dead Letter Queues
concepts.description: Dedicated storage mechanisms for isolating data that repeatedly fails processing, preventing complete pipeline blockages.

concepts.name: Circuit Breaker Pattern
concepts.description: A reliability mechanism that stops calling a failing upstream service to prevent cascading resource exhaustion.

concepts.name: Dataloader Choke Point
concepts.description: An I/O bottleneck where CPU data decoding limits training throughput, leaving the GPU starved for data.

concepts.name: Batch Ingestion
concepts.description: Accumulating and processing data in scheduled, discrete groups to efficiently utilize computational resources.

concepts.name: Stream Ingestion
concepts.description: Processing event data continuously in real-time to minimize latency, requiring always-on infrastructure and complex backpressure handling.

concepts.name: ETL Processing
concepts.description: An architecture that extracts, transforms, and validates data before loading it into storage to enforce strict quality.

concepts.name: ELT Processing
concepts.description: An architecture that loads raw data directly into storage and applies transformations within the target system to maximize experimentation flexibility.

concepts.name: Training-Serving Skew
concepts.description: The silent degradation of model accuracy caused by applying different feature computation logic during training versus production.

concepts.name: Idempotent Transformations
concepts.description: Data processing operations that consistently yield identical outputs given identical inputs, regardless of how many times they execute.

concepts.name: Transformation Lineage
concepts.description: Systematically tracking the sequence, parameters, and versions of code applied to raw data to guarantee reproducibility.

concepts.name: Amdahl's Law for Data
concepts.description: The mathematical limit on speedups achievable through parallelizing distributed data processing, constrained by operations requiring global coordination.
